title: UK Government Announces Social Media Ban for Under-16s
subtitle: New rules aim to protect children's mental health and safety starting in 2027

summary.en: The UK government has announced a plan to ban children under 16 from using major social media platforms. Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated that the law is necessary to protect young people from online harm and bullying. While the ban will not include messaging apps, it will restrict features like livestreaming and contact with strangers. Major technology companies have expressed concern that the ban could push children toward less safe online spaces. The new rules are expected to come into effect in the spring of 2027.
summary.zh: 英國政府宣布了一項計畫，將禁止十六歲以下兒童使用主要社群媒體平台。首相施凱爾表示，這項法律對於保護年輕人免受網路傷害與霸凌是必要的。雖然禁令不包含通訊軟體，但將限制直播及與陌生人聯繫等功能。大型科技公司則表達擔憂，認為禁令可能將兒童推向安全性較低的網路空間。新規定預計於二零二七年春季生效。

paragraphs.en: The UK government has announced a major new policy that will ban children under the age of 16 from accessing most social media platforms. Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer shared the news this week, stating that the law is intended to improve the safety and happiness of young people. According to the BBC, the government hopes to pass this regulation before the end of the year, with the ban officially starting in the spring of 2027.
paragraphs.zh: 英國政府宣布了一項重大新政策，將禁止十六歲以下的兒童使用大多數社群媒體平台。英國首相施凱爾（Keir Starmer）本週分享了這項消息，並表示該法律旨在提升年輕人的安全與幸福感。根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，政府希望在今年年底前通過這項法規，並於二零二七年春季正式實施禁令。

paragraphs.en: The list of platforms affected includes popular apps such as TikTok, Instagram, Snapchat, Facebook, X, and YouTube. However, the government clarified that the ban will not apply to all online services. Messaging applications like WhatsApp and Signal will remain available, as will the online gaming platform Roblox. Additionally, YouTube Kids will be exempt from the new rules. Beyond the total ban, the government is introducing specific restrictions for those under 16, such as blocking the ability to livestream or allowing strangers to contact them. There are also plans to limit features like "infinite scroll," which keeps users on an app for a long time, and to set overnight curfews for users under 18.
paragraphs.zh: 受影響的平台清單包括 TikTok、Instagram、Snapchat、Facebook、X 和 YouTube 等熱門應用程式。不過，政府澄清該禁令並非適用於所有網路服務。像 WhatsApp 和 Signal 這類通訊軟體將維持開放，線上遊戲平台 Roblox 亦然。此外，YouTube Kids 也將豁免於新規定之外。除了全面禁止外，政府也針對十六歲以下的使用者引入了特定限制，例如封鎖直播功能或禁止陌生人與其聯繫。當局也計畫限制如「無限捲動」這類會讓使用者長時間停留在應用程式上的功能，並針對十八歲以下的使用者設定深夜宵禁。

paragraphs.en: Prime Minister Starmer explained that the decision was not made lightly. He noted that social media can make children unhappy and provides tools for bullies to harass others. He argued that the government must choose to prioritize the safety of children over the interests of tech companies. As reported by The Guardian, Starmer compared the ban to existing laws against selling alcohol to minors. He stated that even if some teenagers find ways to break the rules, the law serves as an important expression of national values and will change the way parents and children talk about online safety.
paragraphs.zh: 首相施凱爾解釋，這項決定並非輕率做出。他指出，社群媒體可能導致兒童不快樂，並成為霸凌者騷擾他人的工具。他主張政府必須選擇將兒童的安全置於科技公司的利益之上。據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，施凱爾將此禁令比作現行禁止向未成年人販售酒精的法律。他表示，即便有些青少年會設法規避規則，這項法律仍是國家價值觀的重要體現，並將改變家長與孩子討論網路安全的方式。

paragraphs.en: The announcement has received strong support from various campaigners, including parents who have lost children due to harms linked to social media. Esther Ghey, whose daughter Brianna was killed, told the BBC that she was very happy about the news. She believes the change will help save the lives of many children in the future. Technology Secretary Liz Kendall added in the House of Commons that the government intends to take power away from large tech companies that have failed to keep children safe.
paragraphs.zh: 這項公告獲得了各界倡議人士的強烈支持，其中包括因社群媒體相關傷害而失去孩子的家長。女兒布莉安娜（Brianna）不幸遇害的艾絲特·吉（Esther Ghey）告訴 BBC，她對此消息感到非常高興。她相信這項變革將有助於在未來挽救許多孩子的生命。科技大臣李姿·肯德爾（Liz Kendall）也在下議院補充說明，政府打算從那些未能保護兒童安全的科技巨頭手中收回權力。

paragraphs.en: Despite this support, the plan has faced criticism from major technology firms. Companies like Meta, YouTube, and Snapchat have warned that a blanket ban—a rule that covers everyone without exception—might actually make things worse. According to Meta, banning these platforms risks isolating teenagers from online communities and information. The company argued that such a move would drive young people toward unregulated alternatives that lack parental controls and safety features. YouTube also stated that it provides a beneficial and supervised experience for young people, and that removing access would push them toward less safe services.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管獲得支持，該計畫仍面臨大型科技公司的批評。Meta、YouTube 和 Snapchat 等公司警告，這種「一刀切」的全面禁令可能會讓情況變得更糟。根據 Meta 的說法，禁止這些平台可能會導致青少年與網路社群及資訊隔絕。該公司主張，此舉會將年輕人推向缺乏家長監護與安全機制的非管制替代平台。YouTube 也表示，他們為年輕人提供了有益且受監督的體驗，移除存取權限反而會將他們推向安全性較低的服務。

paragraphs.en: To enforce these new rules, the government plans to require platforms to verify the ages of their users. The communications regulator, Ofcom, will be responsible for deciding exactly how these age-check systems will work. While the government noted that many adults will not need to perform new checks because their accounts are already verified or linked to credit cards, the process for younger users remains a key area of focus. Additionally, the government is targeting AI technology. Under the new rules, AI "romantic companion" chatbots—programs designed to act like a boyfriend or girlfriend—will be restricted for anyone under the age of 18.
paragraphs.zh: 為了執行這些新規定，政府計畫要求平台驗證使用者的年齡。通訊監管機構英國通訊管理局（Ofcom）將負責決定這些年齡驗證系統的具體運作方式。雖然政府指出，許多成年人不需要進行新的驗證，因為他們的帳號已經過驗證或與信用卡連結，但針對年輕使用者的驗證流程仍是重點關注領域。此外，政府也將目標鎖定在人工智慧技術上。根據新規定，旨在扮演男女朋友角色的「浪漫伴侶」人工智慧聊天機器人，將限制十八歲以下者使用。

paragraphs.en: This policy is part of a growing international trend where governments are taking a more active role in regulating the online world. While the government acknowledges that this ban will not solve every problem overnight, supporters see it as a significant step forward. As the country moves toward the 2027 start date, the government has promised to release more details regarding the specific restrictions and the technology required to keep children safe. For now, the focus remains on creating a digital environment that allows children more time and freedom to grow up without the pressures of constant social media use.
paragraphs.zh: 這項政策是全球趨勢的一部分，各國政府正更積極地參與網路世界的監管。雖然政府承認這項禁令無法在一夜之間解決所有問題，但支持者認為這是一個重要的進步。隨著國家邁向二零二七年的實施日期，政府承諾將發布更多關於具體限制以及保護兒童所需技術的細節。目前，重點仍放在創造一個數位環境，讓孩子有更多時間與自由在不受持續使用社群媒體壓力下成長。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Without serious thought or careful consideration.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於描述重量（輕地），這裡指決策過程不草率。文中：Prime Minister Starmer explained that the decision was not made lightly.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Applying to all cases or people without exception.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（毯子），這裡作形容詞修飾禁令。文中：Companies like Meta, YouTube, and Snapchat have warned that a blanket ban—a rule that covers everyone without exception—might actually make things worse.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To force someone or something to move or go in a particular direction.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 迫使、驅使（某人前往某處）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作駕駛（車輛），這裡指迫使青少年轉向其他平台。文中：The company argued that such a move would drive young people toward unregulated alternatives that lack parental controls and safety features.

sources.name: BBC Technology
sources.title: Under-16s will be banned from social media from early 2027

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Social media firms hit back as Starmer announces ban for under-16s in UK

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews

quiz.question: Which of the following is explicitly permitted for users under 16 under the proposed UK government policy?

options.A: Accessing the social media platform X
options.B: Using the online gaming platform Roblox
options.C: Utilizing 'infinite scroll' features on any app
options.D: Engaging with AI romantic companion chatbots

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，政府明確指出遊戲平台 Roblox 將不受禁令影響，因此未成年人仍可使用。選項 A 錯誤，因為 X 被列在受禁令影響的名單中。選項 C 錯誤，文中提到政府正計劃限制此功能。選項 D 錯誤，文章第六段指出 AI 浪漫伴侶聊天機器人將對 18 歲以下青少年進行限制。

quiz.question: Based on the text, what is a primary concern tech companies have regarding the government's 'blanket ban'?

options.A: They believe the government lacks the technical expertise to enforce age checks
options.B: They worry that the ban will cause them to lose all their adult users
options.C: They fear the ban will lead teenagers to use less secure, unregulated online spaces
options.D: They argue that the ban will force them to spend too much money on AI development

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，Meta 與 YouTube 等公司警告，全面禁令可能導致青少年轉向缺乏家長監控與安全機制的「未受監管替代平台」（unregulated alternatives），這顯示科技公司擔心禁令反而會讓青少年暴露在更危險的環境中。選項 A 文中未提及。選項 B 與文章內容不符，禁令主要針對 16 歲以下。選項 D 雖提到 AI，但並非科技公司反對禁令的核心理由。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does Prime Minister Starmer mean when he says the decision was not made 'lightly'?

options.A: The decision was made without enough research or evidence
options.B: The decision was extremely easy to make and required little thought
options.C: The decision was made after serious and careful consideration
options.D: The decision was made to be physically light for the government to carry

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。片語「not made lightly」意指某項決定是經過深思熟慮、嚴肅對待的，而非草率決定。文中提到首相認為此舉是為了保護孩童安全，並將其與禁止販售酒精給未成年人相比，顯示其背後的慎重考量。選項 A 與 B 皆與原意相反。選項 D 則是對該片語的錯誤字面解讀，與決策過程無關。

quiz.question: What is the central purpose of the UK government's new policy regarding social media?

options.A: To completely eliminate all forms of internet usage for people under 18
options.B: To prioritize the safety and well-being of young people by restricting social media access
options.C: To force all tech companies to move their headquarters out of the UK
options.D: To increase the profits of tech companies by forcing them to update their security software

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章開頭第一段即點出政府政策的核心目標是為了提升年輕人的安全與幸福感（improve the safety and happiness of young people）。全文後續段落皆圍繞此目標，討論如何透過限制社交媒體與 AI 互動來達成。選項 A 過度誇大，並非所有網路使用都被禁止。選項 C 與 D 皆非文章提及的政策目的，屬於無中生有的錯誤選項。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
